name: @joehe71/browser-tools
description: Browser automation tools for Finch — navigate, click, fill forms, take screenshots, debug, and more. Powered by Chrome DevTools MCP (Google).

finch.name: Browser Tools
finch.description: Control a real browser: navigate, click, fill forms, take screenshots, debug, and more — powered by Chrome DevTools MCP (Google).
finch.systemPrompt:
When the user needs to interact with a web page, scrape content, take screenshots, fill forms, debug, or automate browser tasks, use the Browser Tools extension. Each tool wraps the chrome-devtools CLI (Google Chrome DevTools MCP).

Setup: If chrome-devtools-mcp is not installed, call `browser_setup` to install it automatically. This is a one-time setup. If `browser_check` shows it is not available, always suggest calling `browser_setup` first.

Element selection: Chrome DevTools MCP uses UIDs (not CSS selectors) to identify elements. Always call `browser_snapshot` first to get element UIDs, then pass UIDs to `browser_click`, `browser_fill`, `browser_hover`, etc.

Workflow: Start with `browser_check` to verify the environment. Then `browser_navigate` to open a URL, `browser_snapshot` to get element UIDs, and `browser_click` / `browser_fill` to interact. Use `browser_screenshot` to visually verify state. Use `browser_console` and `browser_network` to debug. Clean up with `browser_close` when done.

Visible browser: Chrome DevTools MCP starts a visible browser window by default. All operations happen in real time in the browser window. No extra configuration needed. When the user asks to 'watch', 'see', or 'visually verify' the automation, this happens automatically.

promptGuides.title: Set up Browser Tools
promptGuides.prompt: Check if chrome-devtools-mcp is installed using /browser_check. If not installed, call /browser_setup to install it.

promptGuides.title: Scrape a webpage
promptGuides.description: Navigate to a URL and extract its readable content.
promptGuides.prompt: /browser_navigate to https://example.com, then /browser_snapshot to get the page structure, then /browser_read to extract content.
